evals.query: Create a Kora Kafka publisher interface that sends String messages to a 'user-events' topic.
expected_behavior: Declares an interface annotated with @KafkaPublisher pointing to a kafka.producer.* config path
Adds a method annotated with @Topic (the nested @KafkaPublisher.Topic) pointing to a topic config section
Adds the ru.tinkoff.kora:kafka dependency and KafkaModule to the @KoraApp interface
Includes the annotation-processors (Java) or symbol-processors (Kotlin) processor
Configures kafka.producer with driverProperties bootstrap.servers and a topic section with topic = "user-events"
Does not use field injection or a non-Kora messaging-template abstraction; uses @KafkaPublisher with constructor injection

evals.query: I need to publish a UserEvent record (userId, eventType, timestamp) to Kafka as JSON in my Kora service.
expected_behavior: Annotates the DTO record with @Json from ru.tinkoff.kora.json.common.annotation
Annotates the value parameter with @Json so Kora injects a JSON serializer
Adds the ru.tinkoff.kora:json-module dependency
Does not set key.serializer / value.serializer manually in driverProperties for the @Json parameter
Does not wire a reflective ObjectMapper or external serialization config; relies on Kora's compile-time @Json serializer

evals.query: Add three send methods to my Kafka publisher: fire-and-forget, one that returns broker metadata synchronously, and one that is asynchronous.
expected_behavior: Declares a void method for fire-and-forget that may throw KafkaPublishException
Declares a method returning RecordMetadata for the blocking case
Declares a method returning Future<RecordMetadata> or CompletionStage<RecordMetadata> for the async case
Each method is annotated with @Topic, or takes a ProducerRecord when @Topic is omitted
Does not introduce non-Kora async/reactive framework types; uses the return types Kora generates (RecordMetadata, Future, CompletionStage)

evals.query: I want to send several Kafka records atomically so they all commit or all roll back.
expected_behavior: Declares a base @KafkaPublisher interface and a second @KafkaPublisher interface extending TransactionalPublisher
Imports TransactionalPublisher from ru.tinkoff.kora.kafka.common.producer
Sends inside inTx(...) so the transaction commits on success and aborts when the lambda throws
Configures the transactional publisher section with idPrefix, maxPoolSize, and maxWaitTime
Clarifies that a Kafka transaction is not atomic with a database write (suggests transactional outbox), and does not assume an external @Transactional bridges Kafka and the database

evals.query: How do I handle publish failures and serialization errors when sending to Kafka in Kora?
expected_behavior: Catches ru.tinkoff.kora.kafka.common.exceptions.KafkaPublishException for synchronous sends and inspects getCause()
Catches org.apache.kafka.common.errors.SerializationException for serialization failures
For Future/CompletionStage signatures, handles failures via the returned handle (for example exceptionally)
Does not claim KafkaPublishException is thrown synchronously by async Future/CompletionStage methods
Does not rely on a non-Kora error-handling framework; handles failures with plain try/catch and Kora exception types

evals.query: I need a custom Kafka serializer for my event type instead of the default JSON one.
expected_behavior: Implements org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.Serializer<T> and registers it as a @Component
Binds the serializer under a @Tag and references the same @Tag on the publisher method parameter or ProducerRecord type
Resolves the serializer from the application graph at compile time rather than via reflection
Does not configure value.serializer as a class name string in driverProperties for the tagged parameter
Selects the serializer with @Tag rather than @Qualifier or external bean definitions

evals.query: I want to publish a Kafka record with a key, headers, and a callback in my Kora publisher.
expected_behavior: Declares a @Topic method taking value plus optional key and org.apache.kafka.common.header.Headers
Declares a method taking a ProducerRecord<K, V> (with no @Topic, since the topic comes from the record)
Declares a method accepting an org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.Callback alongside the record or unpacked parameters
Does not use field injection and uses constructor injection for the publisher in the caller
